title: sedum album subsp. micranthum
intro: Sedum album subsp. micranthum, also known as White Stonecrop, is a small, succulent plant belonging to the Crassulaceae family, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and thin, fleshy leaves. Traditionally, Sedum album subsp. micranthum has been used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in treating various health issues. The plant's health benefits are said to include soothing digestive problems, reducing fever, and alleviating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Sedum album subsp. micranthum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine for its purported restorative and adaptogenic properties.
